note_patch.B9: Roose (Robb's Harrenhal commander) is suspected of having sent Glover & Tallhart to their destruction at Duskendale to thin the Stark-loyal infantry ahead of his defection. In-text basis: Robb's bafflement signals he never authorized the march ('Why would they go to Duskendale?' / 'A third of my foot, lost for Duskendale?'); the dead were all non-Bolton bannermen while Roose's own Dreadfort men were spared. CORROBORATED by Roose's own words at asos-jaime-05:129 -- 'I gave him all the Karhold men still with me and sent him off with Glover. I do hope nothing ill befell him at Duskendale' (he personally dispatched the rival Karstark men, then voices a soft false concern; quote captured on the battle-at-duskendale node ## Quotes). UNPROVEN in the 5 books (no confession) -- SUSPECTED_OF capped tier-2; fresh-verify-s166 rated this STRONG and undersold.
